---
title: "Bayesian Latent Confounder Model - Complete Data Preparation"
author: "Valiao, Syrelle Kaye D."
date: "2025-11-12"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    code_folding: hide
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: flatly
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

```{r libraries}
library(tidyverse)
library(naniar)
library(kableExtra)
library(readr)
```


# Data Loading and Initial Checks

```{r load_data}
biochem_data <- read_csv("2025-09-05142333_biochemical/2025-09-05142333_data-set_biochemical.csv")
socio_data   <- read_csv("2025-11-18072406_socio/2025-11-18072406_data-set_socio.csv")

cat("Biochemical data: ",  nrow(biochem_data), "rows ×", ncol(biochem_data), "columns\n")
cat("Sociodemographic data:", nrow(socio_data),  "rows ×", ncol(socio_data),  "columns\n")

glimpse(biochem_data)
glimpse(socio_data)
```


# Standardize Keys and Check for Duplicates

```{r standardize_keys}
pad_member <- function(df) {
  df %>% mutate(
    hhnum       = as.character(hhnum),
    member_code = str_pad(as.character(member_code), width = 2, pad = "0", side = "left")
  )
}

biochem_data <- pad_member(biochem_data)
socio_data   <- pad_member(socio_data)

cat("Biochem unique hhnum-member pairs:", n_distinct(biochem_data %>% select(hhnum, member_code)), "\n")
cat("Socio  unique hhnum-member pairs:", n_distinct(socio_data  %>% select(hhnum, member_code)), "\n")

# Identify duplicates
biochem_dupes <- biochem_data %>%
  group_by(hhnum, member_code) %>% filter(n() > 1) %>% ungroup()

socio_dupes <- socio_data %>%
  group_by(hhnum, member_code) %>% filter(n() > 1) %>% ungroup()

cat("\nBiochem duplicates:", nrow(biochem_dupes),
    "| Socio duplicates:", nrow(socio_dupes), "\n")

# Remove duplicates
if (nrow(biochem_dupes) > 0) {
  biochem_data <- biochem_data %>% distinct(hhnum, member_code, .keep_all = TRUE)
  cat("Removed", nrow(biochem_dupes), "duplicate rows from biochem_data\n")
}
if (nrow(socio_dupes) > 0) {
  socio_data <- socio_data %>% distinct(hhnum, member_code, .keep_all = TRUE)
  cat("Removed", nrow(socio_dupes), "duplicate rows from socio_data\n")
}
```


# Merge Datasets (LEFT JOIN on Biochem)

```{r merge}
merged_data <- biochem_data %>%
  left_join(socio_data, by = c("hhnum", "member_code"), suffix = c("_biochem", "_socio"))

cat("Merged data:", nrow(merged_data), "rows ×", ncol(merged_data), "columns\n")
cat("Rows retained from biochem:", nrow(merged_data), "of", nrow(biochem_data), "\n")

# Key matching diagnostic
biochem_keys <- biochem_data %>% select(hhnum, member_code) %>% mutate(in_biochem = TRUE)
socio_keys   <- socio_data   %>% select(hhnum, member_code) %>% mutate(in_socio   = TRUE)
key_check    <- full_join(biochem_keys, socio_keys, by = c("hhnum", "member_code"))

cat("\nOnly in biochem (no socio match):", sum(is.na(key_check$in_socio)),   "\n")
cat("Only in socio  (no biochem match):", sum(is.na(key_check$in_biochem)), "\n")
cat("In BOTH datasets:                 ", sum(!is.na(key_check$in_biochem) & !is.na(key_check$in_socio)), "\n")
```


# Create Urban Indicator

```{r urban_indicator}
# NCR: provhuc 83-99; HUCs: specific provhuc codes
huc_codes <- c(13, 17, 23, 29, 40, 42, 46, 47, 49, 53,
               60, 67, 69, 72, 79, 82, 101, 111)

merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  mutate(
    urban_indicator = case_when(
      provhuc_socio >= 83 & provhuc_socio <= 99 ~ 1,  # NCR
      provhuc_socio %in% huc_codes               ~ 1,  # HUCs
      !is.na(provhuc_socio)                      ~ 0,  # Rural
      TRUE                                       ~ NA_real_
    ),
    norm_wgt = fwgti_natl2_var / mean(fwgti_natl2_var, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

cat("Urban indicator distribution:\n")
print(table(merged_data$urban_indicator, useNA = "ifany"))
```


# Create Analysis Dataset

```{r analysis_dataset}
analysis_data <- merged_data %>%
  rename_with(tolower) %>%
  mutate(
    # --- Outcomes ---
    uic_raw = as.numeric(uic),
    log_uic = ifelse(!is.na(uic_raw) & uic_raw > 0, log(uic_raw), NA_real_),

    # --- Exposure ---
    # NOTE: named `urban` throughout; urban_indicator is the intermediate variable
    urban = as.numeric(urban_indicator),

    # --- Measured Confounders ---
    age       = as.numeric(age),
    sex       = factor(case_when(sex == 1 ~ "Male", sex == 2 ~ "Female",
                                 TRUE ~ NA_character_),
                       levels = c("Male", "Female")),
    education  = as.numeric(educ),
    occupation = factor(occup),
    has_work   = factor(haswork),

    # --- Inflammation Proxies ---
    hemoglobin    = as.numeric(hemoglobin),
    serum_retinol = as.numeric(vita),   # vita = serum retinol in µg/dL

    # --- Spatial Identifiers ---
    province_code   = as.numeric(provhuc_socio),
    region          = factor(regcode_socio),    # used as `region` in EDA
    rep_prov        = as.character(rep_prov_socio),
    regcode_biochem = as.character(regcode_biochem),
    residential_hc  = as.character(rhc),

    # --- Survey Design ---
    psu        = as.character(psc),
    ms_psucode = as.character(ms_psucode_socio),
    weight     = as.numeric(norm_wgt),
    fwgti_prov = as.numeric(fwgti_prov),

    # --- Time ---
    survey_year = as.numeric(enns_year_socio),

    # --- Additional ---
    civil_status = as.character(csc),
    workplace    = as.character(wrkplace)
  ) %>%
  select(
    hhnum, member_code,
    uic_raw, log_uic,
    urban,
    age, sex, education,
    occupation, has_work, civil_status,
    hemoglobin, serum_retinol,
    province_code, region, rep_prov, 
    regcode_biochem, residential_hc,
    psu, ms_psucode, weight, fwgti_prov,
    survey_year, workplace
  )

cat("Final analysis data:", nrow(analysis_data), "rows ×", ncol(analysis_data), "columns\n")
```

# Document Missingness

```{r missingness}
# Overall missingness table
missing_summary <- analysis_data %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "missing_count") %>%
  mutate(
    missing_percent = round(missing_count / nrow(analysis_data) * 100, 2),
    present_count   = nrow(analysis_data) - missing_count,
    present_percent = round(100 - missing_percent, 2)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(missing_count))

missing_summary %>%
  kable(format = "simple", caption = "Missing Data Summary")

# Missingness by variable type
var_groups <- list(
  "Outcome Variables"   = c("uic_raw", "log_uic"),
  "Exposure Variable"   = c("urban"),
  "Measured Confounders" = c("age", "sex", "household_size", "education",
                              "wealth_quintile", "occupation"),
  "Inflammation Proxies" = c("hemoglobin", "serum_retinol",
                              "fever_recent", "diarrhea_recent"),
  "Spatial Identifiers"  = c("province_code", "region"),
  "Survey Design"        = c("psu", "strata", "weight", "survey_year")
)

for (type in names(var_groups)) {
  cat("\n", type, ":\n", sep = "")
  missing_summary %>%
    filter(variable %in% var_groups[[type]]) %>%
    select(variable, missing_count, missing_percent) %>%
    print()
}

# Missingness patterns
cat("\nComplete cases:\n")
cat("  With UIC:              ", sum(!is.na(analysis_data$uic_raw)), "\n")
cat("  With urban:            ", sum(!is.na(analysis_data$urban)),   "\n")
cat("  With UIC + urban:      ",
    sum(!is.na(analysis_data$uic_raw) & !is.na(analysis_data$urban)), "\n")
cat("  All variables complete:", sum(complete.cases(analysis_data)), "\n")

# Visualize missingness (downsampled for speed)
set.seed(0942)
analysis_sample <- analysis_data %>% slice_sample(n = 5000)

gg_miss_upset(analysis_sample %>% select(-hhnum, -member_code),
              nsets = 10, nintersects = 20)

vis_miss(analysis_sample %>% select(-hhnum, -member_code), cluster = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Missingness Pattern (Sample of 5,000 rows)")

library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

missing_summary %>%
  rename(
    "Variable"         = variable,
    "Missing (n)"      = missing_count,
    "Missing (%)"      = missing_percent,
    "Present (n)"      = present_count,
    "Present (%)"      = present_percent
  ) %>%
  kable(
    format    = "latex",
    booktabs  = TRUE,
    caption   = "Missing Data Summary",
    label     = "tab:missingness",
    align     = c("l", "r", "r", "r", "r")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "striped")
  ) %>%
  writeLines("missingness_table.tex")
```


# Descriptive Statistics

```{r descriptive_stats}
# Continuous variables
# BUG FIX: `names_sep = "_"` breaks multi-word variable names (e.g. uic_raw, log_uic).
# Use `names_pattern` to split only on the LAST underscore.
continuous_vars <- c("uic_raw", "log_uic", "age", "hemoglobin", "serum_retinol", "weight")

analysis_data %>%
  select(all_of(continuous_vars)) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), list(
    n      = ~sum(!is.na(.)),
    mean   = ~mean(., na.rm = TRUE),
    sd     = ~sd(., na.rm = TRUE),
    median = ~median(., na.rm = TRUE),
    q25    = ~quantile(., 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    q75    = ~quantile(., 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    min    = ~min(., na.rm = TRUE),
    max    = ~max(., na.rm = TRUE)
  ))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(),
               names_to      = c("variable", "statistic"),
               names_pattern = "^(.+)_([^_]+)$",
               values_to     = "value") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = statistic, values_from = value) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !n, ~round(., 2))) %>%
  kable(format = "simple", caption = "Descriptive Statistics - Continuous Variables")

# Categorical distributions
cat("\nUrban/Rural:\n"); print(table(analysis_data$urban,       useNA = "ifany"))
cat("\nSex:\n");         print(table(analysis_data$sex,         useNA = "ifany"))
cat("\nSurvey Year:\n"); print(table(analysis_data$survey_year, useNA = "ifany"))
```


# Save Outputs

```{r save}
# Single output file — loaded directly by EDA
write_csv(analysis_data, "analysis_data_final.csv")

cat("File saved: analysis_data_final.csv (", nrow(analysis_data), "rows ×",
    ncol(analysis_data), "columns)\n")
```


# Summary Report

```{r summary}
pct <- function(x, n) round(sum(!is.na(x)) / n * 100, 1)
n   <- nrow(analysis_data)

cat("SAMPLE SIZE:\n")
cat("  Original biochem records:", nrow(biochem_data), "\n")
cat("  Final analysis dataset:  ", n, "\n")
cat("  Records retained:        ", round(n / nrow(biochem_data) * 100, 1), "%\n\n")

cat("KEY VARIABLES STATUS:\n")
cat("  Outcome (UIC):    ", sum(!is.na(analysis_data$uic_raw)),      "complete (", pct(analysis_data$uic_raw,      n), "%)\n")
cat("  Exposure (urban): ", sum(!is.na(analysis_data$urban)),        "complete (", pct(analysis_data$urban,        n), "%)\n")
cat("  Age:              ", sum(!is.na(analysis_data$age)),          "complete (", pct(analysis_data$age,          n), "%)\n")
cat("  Sex:              ", sum(!is.na(analysis_data$sex)),          "complete (", pct(analysis_data$sex,          n), "%)\n")
cat("  Hemoglobin:       ", sum(!is.na(analysis_data$hemoglobin)),   "complete (", pct(analysis_data$hemoglobin,   n), "%)\n")
cat("  Serum Retinol:    ", sum(!is.na(analysis_data$serum_retinol)),"complete (", pct(analysis_data$serum_retinol,n), "%)\n")

cat("\nREADY FOR BAYESIAN LATENT CONFOUNDER ANALYSIS\n")
```
